title: My Light Thou Art
content:
My light thou art, without thy glorious sight
My eyes are darkened with eternal night;
My Love, thou art my way, my life, my light.

title: A Ramble In St. James's Park

title: Love And Life
content:
All my past life is mine no more,
The flying hours are gone,
Like transitory dreams giv'n o'er,
Whose images are kept in store
By memory alone.

title: To His Mistress
content:
Why dost thou shade thy lovely face? O why
Does that eclipsing hand of thine deny
The sunshine of the Sun's enlivening eye?

title: The Mistress
content:

Would seem a winter's day;
When life and light, with envious haste,
Are torn and snatched away.

title: A Letter From Artemesia In The Town To Chloe In The Country
content:
Chloe,

In verse by your command I write.
Shortly you'll bid me ride astride, and fight:
These talents better with our sex agree
Than lofty flight

title: Absent Of Thee I Languish Still
content:
Absent from thee I languish still;
Then ask me not, when I return?
The straying fool 'twill plainly kill
To wish all day, all night to mourn.

Dea

title: A Song Of A Young Lady To Her Ancient Lover

title: Epitaph On Charles II
content:
Here lies a great and mighty King,
Whose promise none relied on;
He never said a foolish thing,
Nor ever did a wise one.

title: My Dear Mistress Has A Heart
content:

Soft as those kind looks she gave me,
When with love's resistless art,
And her eyes, she did enslave me;

title: Against Constancy
content:

The frivolous pretense
Of old age, narrow jealousy,
Disease, and want of sense.

content:

That men should fuck in time of flowers,
Or when the smock's beshit.

You shall not keep my heart a day.

title: God Bless Our Good And Gracious King
content:
God bless our good and gracious kind,
Whose promise none relies on,
Who never said a foolish thing,
Nor ever did a wise one.

content:
